methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Supersonic & Hypersonic (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 2 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Boom Supersonic
private.rationale: Same sector (Supersonic & Hypersonic), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Venus Aerospace, a Series B supersonic and hypersonic development company founded by Sassie and Andrew Duggleby, operates within a narrow sector comprising 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 2 private peers (Boom Supersonic, Hermeus). The company has raised $44M+ to date.

Valuation triangulation proved limited by peer scarcity. The Peer-Median method was inapplicable: only 2 same-sector companies exist, insufficient to establish a reliable cohort after filtering for stage alignment and valuation proximity. The Stage-Progression method is applicable and anchors the analysis. Using total raised ($44M) as a proxy for entry valuation and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B progression yields an implied valuation of $84M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $71M to $96M, centered on $84M. This median reflects the stage-typical step-up from deployed capital but carries material uncertainty given the thin comparable set and the nascent commercial stage of supersonic/hypersonic development programs. Key risks to valuation include regulatory certification timelines, capital intensity of prototype maturation, and absence of revenue or near-term path to unit economics. The narrow comp universe limits confidence in market-derived metrics and elevates reliance on funding history as the primary quantitative anchor.
